title: Portable event-loop primitive (epoll / kqueue / IOCP)
goal: Introduce a portable event-loop primitive that wraps epoll on Linux, kqueue on macOS/BSD, and IOCP on Windows. Internally used by file ports, SQLite I/O, subprocess wait, and timers. Public surface stays minimal: make-event-loop, event-loop-add-fd!, event-loop-remove-fd!, event-loop-poll, event-loop-close. v1.4 sockets will register their fds against the same loop.
next_actions: Define inc/eshkol/core/event_loop.h: opaque eshkol_event_loop_t*, eshkol_event_loop_create(int max_events), eshkol_event_loop_add(loop, fd, events, user_data), eshkol_event_loop_remove(loop, fd), eshkol_event_loop_poll(loop, timeout_ms, &out_events, &n_events), eshkol_event_loop_close(loop).
lib/core/event_loop.c: platform dispatch table; per-platform implementation files implement the dispatch entries.
lib/core/event_loop_iocp.c: Windows implementation via CreateIoCompletionPort / GetQueuedCompletionStatus.
Wire into CMakeLists.txt: select platform file based on APPLE/LINUX/WIN32.
Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/event_loop_test.esk: create loop, add a pipe fd, write to its other end, poll, verify the read end is reported readable.
acceptance: Pipe read+write fd round-trip via event-loop-poll completes within timeout.
event-loop-close releases the underlying kqueue/epoll/iocp resource.
ICC `event_loop_works` probe PASS on Darwin AND Linux AND Windows.
verification: ssh jack-blupc 'cd src/eshkol && cmake --build build-jack-msys-ucrt --target eshkol-run && ./build-jack-msys-ucrt/eshkol-run.exe tests/v1_3_edge_cases/event_loop_test.esk -o /tmp/esh0011 && /tmp/esh0011'
v1_4_payoff: v1.4 sockets register their accept/read fds against this loop. v1.4 async I/O is a thin layer on top. v1.4 fibers + channels also schedule against this loop.
